items.hook: After a month-long campaign, the majority of North Korea's Air Force was destroyed by anti-communist forces.
items.body: The Korean People's Army Air Force is the unified military aviation force of North Korea. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Flavours of Iraq review – vivid childhood memories of 80s Baghdad from writer in Paris exile
items.body:
Powerful animation portrays experiences of a French-Iraqi journalist Feurat Alani, with anecdotes about his father, a prominent leftist exile

With a title translated a little inelegantly from Le Parfum d’Irak to “flavours of Iraq”

items.hook: The Odyssey storms global box office with $264m opening
items.body:
Epic film marks Christopher Nolan’s best opening since 2012’s The Dark Knight Rises when adjusted for inflation

The Odyssey has stormed the global box office, taking US$264m (£196m, A$378m) in its opening weekend, according to stud

items.hook: Yellow Pine by Claire Vaye Watkins review – messy portrait of protest subculture
items.body:
Watkins should have been the ideal writer for this sympathetic take on new age spirituality and desert eco-activism – but the results are confusing

items.hook: A huge explosion in the mostly Kurdish border town of Suruç, Turkey, targeting the Socialist Youth Associations Federation, kills at least 31 people and injures over 100.
items.body: On 20 July 2015, the Dokumacılar, the Turkish sect of the Islamic State (ISIL), executed a suicide attack in the Suruç district of Şanlıurfa Province in Turkey. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Godewatta Arachchige Bandula Padmakumara, popularly known as Bandula Padmakumara, was a Sri Lankan journalist and television presenter.
items.body: He was an iconic figure of Sri Lankan media and journalism most notable for the popular television programs Mul Pituwa and Loka Sithiyama. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Sphinxes, movie stars and 3m books: inside Los Angeles’s beloved art deco library

items.hook: A Sudden Flicker of Light by David Thomson review – a brilliantly eccentric history of the movies
items.body:
From the golden age of Hollywood to harder times today, this is a rapturous account of more than a century of cinema

items.hook: A Useful Ghost review – exhilarating black comedy summons ghosts of Thai past via a haunted hoover
items.body:
Ratchapoom Boonbunchachoke’s stylish and unique movie features an unquiet spirit that inhabits a vacuum cleaner, before political interests intervene

items.hook: James Holmes opened fire at a movie theater in Aurora, Colorado, killing 12 and injuring 70 others.
items.body: James Eagan Holmes is an American convicted mass murderer who perpetrated the 2012 Aurora theater shooting in which he killed 12 people and injured 70 others at a Century 16 movie theater on July 20, 2012. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Rampage is a 1963 American adventure film starring Robert Mitchum, Elsa Martinelli and Jack Hawkins.
items.body: Directed by Phil Karlson, it was based on the 1961 novel of the same name by Alan Caillou. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Alex Mochary Kasser is an American politician, attorney and advocate who served as a member of the Connecticut State Senate for the 36th district from 2019 to 2021.
items.body: Connecticut's 36th district covers Greenwich and parts of Stamford and New Canaan. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Intermediate-term memory (ITM) is a stage of memory distinct from sensory memory, working memory/short-term memory, and long-term memory.
items.body: While sensory memory persists for several milliseconds, working memory persists for up to thirty seconds, and long-term memory persists from thirty minutes to the end of an individual's life, intermediate-term memory persists for about two to three hours. Wort

items.hook: Netflix snaps up Danny Boyle’s ‘Ink’ for the US and Latin America
items.body: The feature about Rupert Murdoch’s early newspaper days is set to open the Venice festival in September.

items.hook: The fully restored USS Constitution (a.k.a. Old Ironsides) celebrates its 200th birthday by setting sail for the first time in 116 years.
items.body: USS Constitution, also known as Old Ironsides, is a three-masted wooden-hulled heavy frigate of the United States Navy. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Maciej Ganczar is a Polish literary scholar specializing in German literature, literary translator, author of publications for foreign language teaching, since 2024 Professor at the Medical University of Warsaw.
items.body: One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: In Finland, a new electoral law is ratified, guaranteeing the country the first and equal right to vote in the world. Finnish women are the first in Europe to receive the right to vote.
items.body: Finland, officially the Republic of Finland, is a Nordic country in Northern Europe. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Somali Airlines Flight 40 crashes in Balad, Somalia, killing 50 people.
items.body: On 20 July 1981, Somali Airlines Flight 40, a Fokker F27-600 Friendship operating a daily scheduled domestic passenger flight from Mogadishu International Airport to Hargeisa Airport, Somalia, crashed near the town of Balad a few minutes after takeoff killing all 44 passengers and 6 crew members on board. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.
